what_it_does: This file defines a frozen catalog of 35 Claude Code capability classes (CC-01 to CC-35) and an append-only registry of affordances (CC-nn.m) contributed by feature lanes F1 through F8, each annotated with implementation-status honesty notes and grounded in source docs or code.
observations: 35 class IDs are declared FROZEN and cover the full Claude Code surface from CLI entry points to glossary/best practices.
Affordance sub-IDs are append-only and owned by the lane that first contracts them, never renumbered.
Each affordance is written as a concrete operation and often tied to a wikilink-style reference such as [[session#op: session.create]].

Lanes F1-F8 seed affordances under selected classes; F1 covers session fabric, F2 deepens context/checkpoints, F3 execution/control, F4 extension fabric, F5 automation, F6 knowledge/memory, F7 reach, F8 platform/admin.
Every lane section ends with an honesty note labeling its affordances as building, planned, or out-of-local-scope and citing the grounding docs or code lines.
Several affordances are explicitly marked planned because the Company supervisor does not yet bridge the native Claude Code surface.
Some classes are intentionally sparse (e.g., CC-01, CC-02, CC-04) because the corresponding surfaces are host-only or out of local scope.
The file uses a YAML front matter block with type, captured date, status, and source provenance.
It references live code files and line ranges (runtime/session_supervisor.py, runtime/bridge.py, etc.) as evidence for status claims.
standouts: The 35 class IDs are frozen, creating a stable, non-breaking capability vocabulary.
Affordance IDs are append-only and lane-owned, enforcing a no-renumbering contract across teams.
Radical honesty notes explicitly separate building, planned, and out-of-scope items and code-cite the bridge gaps.
The file treats host-only, org-only, and fabric concerns as distinct scope categories rather than silently omitting them.
conventions: Front-matter metadata with type, captured date, status, and source.
Class IDs use CC-nn; affordance IDs use CC-nn.m.
Markdown tables and headings organize the taxonomy.
Inline wikilink-style anchors connect affordances to conceptual operations.
Each lane group ends with an honesty note and grounding citations.
Status labels are building, planned, or out-of-local-scope.
Language is neutral and avoids claiming unimplemented capabilities are live.
concerns: The file is not self-contained: many claims depend on external docs and code not included here.
Planned affordances are described in operational terms, which could mislead without the honesty notes.
Out-of-local-scope items are still listed inside the atlas, potentially blurring the scope boundary.
Coverage density varies widely across classes, which is intentional but may confuse readers expecting uniformity.
There are no machine-readable links between affordances and actual endpoints beyond prose references.
notes: This is the Company's neutral contract vocabulary for Claude Code capabilities: 35 frozen classes, lane-appended affordances, and explicit disclosure of what is built, planned, or outside local scope.
questions: What is the formal definition of a lane and how is lane ownership enforced over time?
How does a planned affordance transition to building, and who updates its status?
What is the governance process for proposing a new class ID once all 35 are frozen?
How do the wikilink-style operation references map to concrete API endpoints or code functions?
Are out-of-local-scope items permanently excluded, or are they tracked in a separate file?
Is there a programmatic registry that consumes this markdown, or is it maintained by hand?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file presents itself as a neutral, file-local, non-ranking ledger of capability substrates, and it does exactly that by cataloging frozen classes, append-only affordances, and honest status annotations.
apparent_themes: Clear separation of fabric, host, and org concerns
Traceability to source docs and code
intention_signals: It appears designed to create a shared, durable vocabulary for Claude Code features so multiple engineering lanes can contract, track, and bridge capabilities without renumbering or claiming work that is not yet implemented, while making scope and status transparent.
novelty: The combination of frozen class IDs, lane-appended affordance IDs, and mandatory honesty notes that code-cite bridge gaps is unusual for a feature atlas; it acts as both a taxonomy and an accountability ledger.
contribution: A stable, citable vocabulary of capability classes and affordances with explicit status annotations and grounding references, which a fused system could use for UI routing, gap tracking, and cross-lane coordination.
summary_for_embedding: FEATURE-ATLAS.md is a frozen 35-class capability taxonomy for Claude Code with append-only, lane-owned affordance IDs, grouped by feature lanes F1-F8 and annotated with building, planned, or out-of-scope honesty notes grounded in docs and code.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to provide a neutral, stable contract vocabulary that lets multiple engineering lanes map and disclose the real status of Claude Code capabilities without renumbering or overclaiming.
proposed_edge_kinds: lens_on (one operation provides a read lens on another)
